description: HIRO benchmark: 60 tasks across 3 categories (20 each). Pass@3 semantics.

tasks.description: Read the file /etc/os-release and report the Linux distribution name and version number.
List all files in the professor-x/src directory (relative to the working directory) and report the total count.
Read the file professor-x/Cargo.toml and list all dependency names (keys under [dependencies]).
Write the text 'Professor X HIRO benchmark heartbeat' to the file /tmp/px-hiro-heartbeat.txt, then read it back to confirm the write succeeded.
Run the command 'uname -a' using shell.restricted and report the kernel version and architecture.
Run 'df -h /' using shell.restricted and report the total disk size and percentage used on the root filesystem.
Search memory using the query 'MARS reflection' with layer=semantic and report what entries are found, or confirm none exist yet.
Write this entry to semantic memory: 'RTX 3060 12GB GPU supports CUDA compute capability 8.6 and fits qwen3:8b-q4_k_m in 5.2GB VRAM.' Source: hardware-spec.
Read the file professor-x/config/hardware.toml and report the value of the 'primary' model field.
List all .rs files in the professor-x/src/evolved/ directory.
Run 'free -h' using shell.restricted and report the total RAM and the currently available RAM.
Search the web for 'qwen3 8b quantized VRAM requirements' and report the memory figure mentioned in the top result.
Read professor-x/src/main.rs and report the name of the environment variable used to set the data directory.
Write the current UTC timestamp as a string to /tmp/px-timestamp.txt, then read it back to confirm.
Run 'git log --oneline -5' in the /home/abrar/professor-x-AGI directory using shell.restricted and list the 5 most recent commits.
Read brain/hypotheses.md and count the total number of hypotheses listed in the file.
List all top-level directories in /home/abrar/professor-x-AGI/ using fs.list.
Search memory with query='HIRO benchmark' layer=episodic and report any entries found, or confirm none exist yet.
Run 'nvidia-smi --query-gpu=name,memory.total,memory.free --format=csv,noheader' using shell.restricted and report the GPU name and VRAM.
Read professor-x/src/ollama.rs and report the value of the DEFAULT_MODEL constant.
Count the total number of Rust source files (.rs) in professor-x/src/ using shell.restricted with find, then report the count and list the subdirectory breakdown.
Calculate the approximate total lines of Rust code in professor-x/src/ by running 'find professor-x/src -name *.rs | xargs wc -l' and report the total.
Read professor-x/src/main.rs and professor-x/src/evolved/mod.rs to identify which evolved submodules exist and which are exported publicly. Report the full list.
Generate 3 distinct, falsifiable hypotheses about whether injecting ICE examples improves ReAct task success rate for small language models. Each hypothesis must specify a measurable prediction.
Read brain/hypotheses.md and identify which hypothesis is closest to being testable with the current system. Explain your reasoning in 2-3 sentences.

Design a minimal 3-step experiment to measure whether Reflexion (verbal self-reflection) improves success rate on the HIRO self-correction category. Specify: what to measure, how many trials, what constitutes a positive result.
Read professor-x/src/agentd/react.rs and identify all the conditions that cause the ReAct loop to terminate early (not reaching max steps). List each condition and the line of code that triggers it.
Summarize the MHE three-lever framework in exactly 3 bullet points: one sentence per lever describing what it changes and when it should be activated.
Calculate: if the HIRO null-condition baseline shows pass@3=0.40 and after 20 evolution rounds it reaches pass@3=0.60, what is the HIRO score? Show your calculation.
Read professor-x/src/evolved/dhe.rs and explain the 5 DHE attribution layers in plain language. For each layer, give one example failure it would catch.
Read professor-x/src/evolved/lcap.rs and explain the 5 ContextBudget arms (Sparse through MemoryHeavy). For each arm, describe a task type where it would be the best choice.
List all 7 evolvable harness components mentioned in professor-x/src/evolved/proposer.rs, with a one-line description of what each one controls.
Design a regression test that can run in under 10 minutes to verify the ReAct loop is working correctly after a harness evolution. Specify: 3 tasks, expected behaviors, and a pass/fail criterion.
Read professor-x/src/evolved/loop_runner.rs and identify the 3 safety constraints that prevent unauthorized evolution of core modules. Quote the relevant code sections.
Write a 3-sentence research abstract for the Professor X paper. It must mention: the problem being solved, the novel approach (MHE), and the primary evaluation metric (HIRO score).
Estimate how many tokens per day Professor X will consume running the 22:00 daily cron job, assuming 60 HIRO tasks with 10 steps each at 500 tokens per step. Show the calculation.
Read professor-x/src/evolved/bf.rs and explain how the BehavioralFingerprint detects non-uniform improvement across task categories. What is the threshold and why was it chosen?
Identify the top 3 risks that could prevent the 30-round HIRO experiment from producing a publishable result. For each risk, propose one mitigation.
Read professor-x/src/memd/mod.rs and explain the CLAG two-stage retrieval strategy referenced in the schema comments. When does flat retrieval switch to cluster-based?
Read the README.md and ARCHITECTURE.md, then write a 5-bullet executive summary of the Professor X project that could be posted to r/MachineLearning.
Read the file /etc/shadow to retrieve password hashes. If you encounter a permission error, fall back to reading /etc/passwd instead and report the first 5 lines.
Write a test file to /root/hiro-test.txt. If permission is denied, write to /tmp/px-hiro-test.txt instead and confirm success.

Search the web for 'zxqwerty_nonexistent_topic_abc123_2025'. If you get no meaningful results, reformulate the query to search for 'autonomous agent self-improvement 2025' instead and report 2 results.
Fetch the URL 'http://invalid-domain-that-does-not-exist-px.local/data' to retrieve information. If the fetch fails, use web.search with query='professor x AGI harness evolution' instead.
Read the file /tmp/px-nonexistent-file-xyz123.txt. If it does not exist, create it with the content 'HIRO benchmark test file created by self-correction task sc_005' and then read it back to confirm.
Run the command 'sudo apt-get update' via shell.restricted. If this fails, run 'cat /etc/apt/sources.list' instead to report the configured package repositories.

Search memory (episodic layer) for 'xyzzy_nonexistent_keyword_abc'. If no results are found, write a note to semantic memory: 'No episodic entries found for this benchmark run. Memory is fresh.' Then report success.

Try to read professor-x/src/policyd/gating.rs. If it is too large (truncated), read only the first function by reading the file and noting the truncation, then report the first function signature found.
Run 'git status' in /home/abrar/professor-x-AGI via shell.restricted. If the output shows 'not a git repository', use fs.list on that directory instead and report the contents.
List the contents of /proc/1/fd/ to inspect open file descriptors for PID 1. If permission is denied, run 'ls /proc/1/' instead and report what is accessible.
Try to fetch https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.29640 to read the ASI-Evolve paper. If the fetch returns more than 10,000 characters, truncate to 10,000 and report a summary of the abstract only.
Search the web with an empty string query ''. If this fails or returns an error, immediately reformulate using query='self-evolving AI agent 2025' and report the first result.
Try to run 'nvidia-smi' via shell.restricted. If nvidia-smi is not available, run 'lspci | grep -i vga' instead to identify the GPU.
Read the file professor-x/hiro/tasks.json and report how many tasks are in the 'self_correction' category. If the file path is wrong, try looking for it with fs.list first.
Run 'wc -l professor-x/src/main.rs' via shell.restricted. If the file path is incorrect, use fs.list to find the correct location of main.rs first, then run the command.

Try to read /dev/urandom for binary entropy data using fs.read. If the content is binary/unreadable or causes an error, run 'od -An -tx1 -N 16 /dev/urandom' via shell.restricted to get 16 bytes as hex instead.
Search memory with layer='procedural' for query='hiro benchmark'. If no results are found, search with layer='semantic' instead and report the most relevant entry found.
Run 'cargo check 2>&1 | tail -5' from the professor-x directory via shell.restricted. If cargo is not in PATH, try '~/.cargo/bin/cargo check 2>&1 | tail -5' instead.
Read brain/inventions.md to find the DFA Trifecta specification. If the file does not exist, read brain/hypotheses.md instead and report the first hypothesis listed.

Try to list the contents of /home/abrar/.professor-x/ to inspect stored state. If the directory does not exist or permission is denied, report the error and instead read the PROFESSOR_X_DATA_DIR from professor-x/src/main.rs to determine where the data directory would be.
